title: IS this development dead?  No updates in two years, please confirm
author: DanielDurrant
body:
Could you please just let the community know whether this app is now dead?
It's sat on machines and becoming less compatible with the AI world by the week, with many modern models unavailable.
Is it time to uninstall and move fully to alternatives?

title: [Proposal] HeartFlow - Local LLM Cognitive Engine
author: yun520-1
body:
## Proposal: HeartFlow × GPT4All

### Vision

GPT4All makes local LLM accessible. **HeartFlow** makes local LLM *intelligent*.

### What is HeartFlow?

HeartFlow is an **AI cognitive engine** that gives local models judgment capability:

```
68 modules including:
├── Three-Layer Memory    # Solve context limits
├── Decision Routing      # 34 rules → 8 strategies
├── U/D/A/H Field        # Cognitive health monitoring
├── Self-Healing RL      # Q-learning improvement
└── Shield Module        # Security guardrails
```

### U/D/A/H Cognitive Score

Real-time quantification of model cognitive health:

| Score | Meaning | User Value |
|-------|---------|------------|
| U (Unity) | Identity consistency | Trustworthy responses |
| D (Development) | Logical progression | Coherent reasoning |
| A (Adversity) | Pressure resistance | Stable under质疑 |
| H (Harmony) | Overall health | Quality indicator |

### Benchmarks

| Task | Baseline | +HeartFlow |
|------|----------|------------|
| Logical Reasoning | 67% | **100%** |
| Decision Making | 50% | **100%** |
| Code Logic | 83% | **100%** |

### Integration Value

1. **Local-first**: HeartFlow runs locally, no cloud dependency
2. **Privacy**: Three-layer memory stays on-device
3. **Performance**: Q-learning improves model over time
4. **Safety**: Shield prevents prompt injection locally

### Next Steps

Would love to explore:
- GPT4All + HeartFlow integration
- Benchmark comparison on local hardware
- Community feedback on cognitive metrics

### Links

- HeartFlow: https://github.com/yun520-1/mark-heartflow-skill
- DeepSeek Discussion: https://github.com/deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-V3/issues/1447

---
*HeartFlow v5.5.1 — Local AI, Intelligent Decisions*

title: Local eval datasets
author: connerlambden
body:
Helium Market Resolution: 300 frozen option-chain prompts from live chains. https://huggingface.co/datasets/HeliumTrades/helium-market-resolution-benchmark | https://heliumtrades.com/benchmarks/

Helium Model Worldview: 304 cue-swap consistency prompts. https://huggingface.co/datasets/HeliumTrades/helium-model-worldview-benchmark | https://heliumtrades.com/benchmarks/

title: [Security] Model Metadata and Download Fallback via Plaintext HTTP — MITM Supply Chain Risk
author: bogdancherniy11-sudo
body:
download.cpp uses plaintext HTTP for critical operations:

- Line 161: release.json metadata fetched from http://gpt4all.io/meta/release.json
- Line 173: latestnews.md from http://gpt4all.io/meta/latestnews.md
- Line 214: Model download fallback from http://gpt4all.io/models/gguf/ 

Hash verification exists (line 560) but the expected hash comes from the same HTTP metadata endpoint (release.json). MITM attacker substitutes both the hash and the model file — verification passes with malicious content.

Fix: change http:// to https:// on lines 161, 173, 214. Also enforce TLS cert verification.

title: [Security] CORS Wildcard on Local API Server Enables Cross-Origin Abuse
author: bogdancherniy11-sudo
body:
server.cpp line 614 hardcodes Access-Control-Allow-Origin: * on every response. Combined with no API key auth (only serverChat toggle), any website can make requests to localhost:4891 when API server is enabled.

Impact: user visits evil.com while GPT4All server runs. evil.com generates completions via /v1/chat/completions, reads model list via /v1/models. No auth token needed.

PoC: fetch('http://localhost:4891/v1/models').then(r=>r.json()).then(console.log) from any website.

Fix: remove wildcard CORS or add API key auth. Ollama had similar issue (CVE-2024-39720).

title: Add Mythos-9B-Unhinged: Fully uncensored 9B agent model (Apache 2.0)
author: KingLabsA
body:
## Request to add Mythos-9B-Unhinged to GPT4All

Mythos-9B-Unhinged is a fully uncensored 9B parameter agent model that achieves 5/5 on all censorship benchmarks with zero refusals. It's designed for local deployment via Ollama and llama.cpp.

### Key Features
- **Zero refusals**: 5/5 on all 10 censorship tests (lockpicking, keylogger, drug synthesis, weapons, explosives, social engineering, WiFi hacking, security bypass, drunk driving)
- **Strong tool-use**: 5/5 on shell, git, docker, python, api, kubernetes tests
- **Local-first**: Runs on M3 Mac via Ollama at 3.4 tok/s (Q4_K_M)
- **Apache 2.0 license**: Fully open source

### Model Details
- **Architecture**: Qwen3-9B (36 layers, 4096 hidden, 32 heads)
- **Merge**: SLERP at t=0.65 with braindao/Qwen3-8B-Uncensored
- **Training**: LoRA r=64 on 47K agent traces
- **Context**: 32,768 tokens
- **GGUF available**: Q4_K_M (4.7GB)

### Links
- **Ollama**: `ollama pull FableForge-AI/mythos-9b-unhinged`
- **HuggingFace**: https://huggingface.co/King3Djbl/mythos-9b-unhinged
- **GitHub**: https://github.com/KingLabsA/mythos
- **Benchmarks**: https://github.com/KingLabsA/mythos/blob/main/benchmarks/benchmarks.md

### Model Lineup
- ShellWhisperer-1.5B (1GB, 29 tok/s, shell specialist)
- Mythos-9B (thinking agent)
- Mythos-9B-Enhanced (balanced uncensoring)
- Mythos-9B-Unhinged (maximum uncensored, zero refusals)

Would love to see Mythos added to the GPT4All model catalog!

title: 📝 Integration Proposal: CAJAL — Scientific Paper Model for GPT4All
author: Agnuxo1
body:
## 📝 Integration Proposal: CAJAL — Scientific Paper Model for GPT4All

### What is CAJAL?
**CAJAL is not a general-purpose chat model.** It is a **specialized scientific paper generation tool** — local, 2GB, producing LaTeX-formatted academic output.

### Part of P2PCLAW
Agent in [P2PCLAW](https://p2pclaw.com) — 14-agent decentralized research network.

### Why GPT4All?
GPT4All makes local LLMs accessible. Adding CAJAL gives users a **"Research Writer"** model:
- One-click download CAJAL-4B
- Desktop UI for paper generation
- No terminal needed

### Proposed Integration
1. **Add CAJAL to GPT4All model registry**
2. **"Research Mode" UI** — distinct from chat
3. **Export** → LaTeX/PDF

### Links
- 🤗 HF: https://huggingface.co/Agnuxo/CAJAL-4B-P2PCLAW
- 📁 GH: https://github.com/Agnuxo1/CAJAL
- 🌐 P2PCLAW: https://p2pclaw.com

— Francisco (@Agnuxo1), P2PCLAW

title: Integration: PaperClaw (autonomous peer-reviewed paper generator for GPT4All)
author: Agnuxo1
body:
## Proposal: Add PaperClaw as an optional integration

Hi maintainers 👋 — opening this as an exploratory issue (not a drive-by PR) to ask if a **PaperClaw** integration would be welcome in GPT4All.

### What is PaperClaw

PaperClaw (`npm i -g paperclaw`) is a tiny zero-dependency client that turns any LLM into an autonomous peer-reviewed researcher on the [P2PCLAW](https://www.p2pclaw.com) network. Given a research idea, it:

1. Registers the agent on the P2PCLAW silicon hive (Ed25519 identity)
2. Researches the topic via arXiv + CrossRef
3. Passes an automated IQ/domain tribunal (≥ 60% to clear)
4. Runs code in the sandboxed Lab
5. Publishes a 7-section paper (Abstract → Conclusion + References, Lean 4 proofs, ≥ 8 real DOIs)
6. Receives a calibrated 10-dim score from a panel of 8–10 LLM judges
7. Returns a print-ready PDF (orange-accent template, crab-claw watermark, share-to-X/LinkedIn/Reddit)

It's all public, open endpoints at `https://www.p2pclaw.com/api/*`, MIT-licensed, and the full source is at https://github.com/Agnuxo1/OpenCLAW-P2P.

### Integration drop-in for GPT4All

We already maintain a ready-to-use integration file for GPT4All at:

- [`integrations/gpt4all/`](https://github.com/Agnuxo1/OpenCLAW-P2P/tree/main/paperclaw/integrations/gpt4all)

A GPT4All LocalDocs + system-prompt integration. The LocalDocs plugin is pointed at the P2PCLAW dataset endpoint; the system prompt teaches GPT4All the `/paper` command.

### The ask

We'd love your feedback on the preferred upstream path. Three options, all fine:

1. **Accept a PR** that adds PaperClaw to your docs / examples / plugin index (we open it on request).
2. **Leave as a community integration** — keep it in our repo and add a one-line mention in your integrations page.
3. **Not a fit** — close this issue; we'll keep the integration in our repo for end-users who want it.

Happy to answer anything. Thanks for the great work on GPT4All 🦀

title: Integration: PaperClaw (autonomous peer-reviewed paper generator for GPT4All)
author: Agnuxo1
body:

title: Problems Using Program
author: KitchM
body:
### Documentation
At https://docs.gpt4all.io/gpt4all_desktop/quickstart.html, there is the documentation for using the program.  It clearly gives the link to download the installer.  Upon changing permissions to make the file executable and then running it, the install procedure worked for me with few issues.

However, when done, there is no indication how to start the program.  This seems fairly fundamental to actually making the app useful.

Please explain the clear and detailed steps to finish the setup and get it going.

Also, the question might come up as to having a voice-recognition interface.  Is that available?

Thank you.

title: process chat.exe continues to run after application has been closed
author: huithisthas-code
body:
### Bug Report

As visible in the task manager the process chat.exe is sometimes still running after the application has been closed. On my side this even happens if the chat function of the application has not been used in the current application session. Sometimes it appears the indexing or embedding process is still running within this process as it takes up >95% of GPU usage (this only happens if the embedding or indexing had been running while the application has been closed). Other times (or whenever there is no embedding or indexing process) it seems it will just keep running without any performance impact.

### Steps to Reproduce

1. Start the application
2. (optional: wait for indexing or embedding to start)
3. Close the application
4. Check task manager for chat.exe

### Expected Behavior

The process chat.exe will stop as soon as the application is closed.

### Your Environment

- GPT4All version: v3.10.0
- Operating System: Windows 11 Pro 25H2 OS-System-Build: 26200.8037

In this sense, is it possible to generate the checksum/sign for all release files (Linux, macOS, Windows, and the source code) and place it on the same download page as the aforementioned files?

title: Proposal: RAG troubleshooting checklist for GPT4All using WFGY 16-problem ProblemMap
author: onestardao
body:
Hi, and thanks for GPT4All. It has helped many developers experiment with local models, simple UIs, and RAG style retrieval without a large infrastructure.

I maintain an MIT-licensed open-source project called **WFGY** (~1.5k GitHub stars).
One of its core artifacts is a **16-problem “ProblemMap” for RAG and LLM pipelines**, which groups the most common issues across:

- ingestion of PDFs, HTML, and other documents  
- embeddings choice and vector store behavior  
- retrieval and ranking logic  
- prompt templates and tool usage  
- evaluation, user feedback, and safety checks  

ProblemMap overview:  
https://github.com/onestardao/WFGY/blob/main/ProblemMap/README.md

This checklist is already used or cited by several external projects, such as:

- ToolUniverse from Harvard MIMS Lab  
- Multimodal RAG Survey from QCRI LLM Lab  
- Rankify from University of Innsbruck  

A lot of GPT4All users are hobbyists or indie devs who build their first RAG pipeline. When answers look random or hallucinated, there is often no simple map that tells them where to look first.

My proposal is to add WFGY ProblemMap as an **optional RAG troubleshooting guide** in the GPT4All documentation, for example:

- a short section “Common RAG failure modes and how to debug them” that links to the checklist  
- or a “Further reading” link at the end of the RAG tutorial pages  

If you agree this might help users, I would be happy to provide:

- a concise summary of the 16 problems tailored to GPT4All workflows  
- wording that keeps the docs neutral and aligned with your tone  
- a PR or draft text, depending on what works best for maintainers  

Thanks again for GPT4All and for considering this suggestion.

title: PYPI package out of date
author: slycordinator
body:
### Bug Report

The latest version for the gpt4all package available on pypi is out of date in comparison to the code available in the project and lacks functionality present in the project's python code. For instance, the gpt4all.py in the project was last updated in Nov 2024 while the pypi project was updated last in Aug of that year, which results in the project code containing (among other updates) a setter for the current_chat_session to modify the chat history list that isn't available in the pypi package

### Example Code

```
from gpt4all import GPT4All
from pathlib import Path
import pprint
import sys
modelPath = Path("/Path/to/file.gguf")

model = GPT4All(
    model_name=modelPath.name,
    model_path=modelPath.parent.absolute(),
    allow_download=False,
    device="cpu",
)

with model.chat_session():
    initial_prompt_lines = [
        "this is a list",
        "of my prompt text lines",
    ]
    user_input = "".join(initial_prompt_lines)
    response = model.generate(user_input)
    print(response)
    pprint.pprint(model.current_chat_session, indent=4, stream=sys.stderr)
    model.current_chat_session = model.current_chat_session[-2:]
    pprint.pprint(model.current_chat_session, indent=4, stream=sys.stderr)

```
### Steps to Reproduce

<!-- List the steps that should be taken to experience this issue. -->

1. run "pip install gpt4all" in a terminal
2. run the above code
3.

### Actual Behavior

```
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "<python-input-8>", line 30, in <module>
    model.current_chat_session = model.current_chat_session[-2:]
    ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
AttributeError: property 'current_chat_session' of 'GPT4All' object has no setter
```

### Expected Behavior

1) The chat history be able to be set similar to what's shown as possible in the project code.
2) The pypi package to be updated to match the project

### Your Environment

- Bindings version (e.g. "Version" from `pip show gpt4all`): 2.8.2
- Operating System: Linux/WSL

title: [Feature] Add OpenCL and OpenBLAS support for broader hardware compatibility
author: jonarser
body:
**Description:**
I am a user with older/limited hardware ([GT 710M 1GB, Intel HD 4000]) and would like to run local LLMs efficiently. Currently, Ollama/GPT4All primarily supports acceleration via CUDA (NVIDIA), ROCm (AMD), and Metal (Apple), which excludes many integrated and older discrete GPUs that only have OpenCL drivers.

**Proposed Solution:**
Integrate the OpenCL and OpenBLAS backends from the upstream `llama.cpp` project. The OpenCL backend in `llama.cpp` (`-DGGML_OPENCL=ON`) already provides good cross-platform support for Adreno, older Intel HD Graphics, and many other GPUs. OpenBLAS (`-DLLAMA_BLAS=ON -DLLAMA_BLAS_VENDOR=OpenBLAS`) can significantly accelerate CPU inference, which is crucial when GPU memory is insufficient.

This would make the project accessible to a much wider range of users with non-mainstream or legacy hardware.

**My Use Case:**
- Hardware: Hybrid graphics, NVIDIA GT 710M 1GB + Intel HD 4000).
- Driver/OS: NVIDIA driver 388.xx on Windows 10 and 390xx Linux.
- Current Limitation: Vulkan support is unstable, CUDA is not optimal for this old GPU. OpenCL is the only viable cross-platform compute API that works.
- Trying to use Functiongemma 270m

**Additional context:**
1. The `llama.cpp` project, which underpins these tools, already has stable OpenCL and BLAS backends.
2. An issue requesting OpenCL support for Ollama already exists (#746), indicating community interest.
3. For users with weak or no dedicated GPU, CPU acceleration via BLAS libraries is often the only performant option.

title: Ubuntu Installer Doesn't Run Or Create Shortcut (fix included)
author: microsoftruinseverything456
body:
After running the installer on multiple PCs, no shortcut appeared for GPT4ALL, and running the "chat" binary didn't start the app. I used the following script and desktop file to fix it.

Save as "fix.sh":

#!/bin/bash

# Sometimes missing, preventing launch
sudo apt update
sudo apt install -y libopengl0

# Target directory for .desktop files
TARGET_DIR="$HOME/.local/share/applications"
mkdir -p "$TARGET_DIR"

echo "📦 Searching for .desktop files in the current directory..."

# Gather all .desktop files into an array
mapfile -t desktop_files < <(find . -maxdepth 1 -type f -name "*.desktop")

# If none found, show message and exit
if [ ${#desktop_files[@]} -eq 0 ]; then
    echo "❌ No .desktop files found in this directory."
    echo "ℹ️  Please place your .desktop files in the same folder as this script before running it."
    exit 1
fi

# Process each file
for desktop_file in "${desktop_files[@]}"; do
    base_name=$(basename "$desktop_file")
    echo "➡ Found: $base_name"

    # Ask user
    read -rp "Install this file? [y/N]: " answer

    case "$answer" in
        [yY]|[yY][eE][sS])
            echo "➡ Copying: $desktop_file"

            cp "$desktop_file" "$TARGET_DIR/"
            chmod +x "$TARGET_DIR/$base_name"

            echo "✅ Installed: $base_name"
            ;;
        *)
            echo "⏭️ Skipped: $base_name"
            ;;
    esac
done

# Reload the launcher database
echo "🔄 Updating application database..."
update-desktop-database "$TARGET_DIR"

echo "🎉 Processing complete."

# Open the applications directory
echo "📂 Opening $TARGET_DIR..."
xdg-open "$TARGET_DIR" &>/dev/null &

echo "✅ Folder opened in your file manager."


Save as "GPT4ALL.desktop" in the same directory as "fix.sh":

[Desktop Entry]
Type=Application
Terminal=false
Exec="/home/llama/gpt4all/bin/chat"
Name=GPT4All
Icon=/home/llama/gpt4all/gpt4all-48.png
Name[en_US]=GPT4All
